projectDescription: A Spanish-language interactive React app that teaches children numbers 1–11 through three engaging modes: step-by-step learning, multiple-choice practice, and freehand drawing on a canvas.
keyFeatures: Step-by-step number learning (1–11) with emoji visual aids and color-coded cards
Multiple-choice practice mode with randomly generated answer options
Freehand canvas writing mode with ghost-number guide for tracing
Random mode for advanced learners that shuffles number order
Star reward system that tracks correct answers across sessions
Animated feedback overlays (¡Genial! / Casi...) using Framer Motion
Fully responsive layout with Tailwind CSS, works on touch devices
Deployable to GitHub Pages via gh-pages
publicApiSurface: NUMBERS_DATA array (val, word, emoji, color per number 1–11)
App component — root React component managing view state
Menu view — main navigation with Paso a Paso and Modo Aleatorio buttons
LearnView — displays current number with emoji grid and navigation buttons
PracticeView — multiple-choice quiz showing emoji objects to count
WritingView — canvas-based number tracing with ghost guide
startLearning(mode) — initialises learning session in 'linear' or 'random' mode
nextNumber() — advances to next number (linear) or random number
handleCorrect() — awards a star and advances after 1.5 s delay
stars state — cumulative correct-answer counter displayed in nav bar
